§6Modular Storage: Powercell (Medium)§f

§aScale up your RF storage network with the mid-tier modular Powercell!§f

The §9Powercell (Medium)§f is the second-tier energy storage block from §6RFTools Power§f. It functions identically to the lower tier but offers a massive leap in storage and transfer capabilities, serving as the perfect backbone for mid-game power distribution.

§6Powercell Specifications§f:
§b• Storage Capacity§f: Holds up to §e4,000,000 RF§f of internal energy buffer (8× more than Low).
§b• Transfer Capacity§f: Limits energy transfer to §c1,000 RF/tick§f per side under default conditions.
§b• Network Scaling§f: Integrates seamlessly into your existing multiblock network (up to §c729 blocks§f total). Every additional cell increases the network's maximum side transfer rate by a factor of §a+0.25× (25%)§f!
§b• NBT Preservation§f: When broken, the block §aretains all stored energy§f, allowing safe relocation without losing power.

§6Upgrading & Configuration§f:
§b• Side I/O Mode§f: §9Right-click§f a face with a §9wrench§f or §9Smart Wrench§f to toggle between §bInput§f (blue), §cOutput§f (orange), and §7Disabled§f (none).
§b• Upgrade Continuity§f: Crafted in a §6Copy NBT recipe§f. If your §bPowercell (Low)§f used in the upgrade contains RF, §a100% of that energy is preserved§f in the resulting Medium cell!
§b• Wireless Linking§f: Install a §bPowercell Card§f inside the slot to wirelessly link this cell's storage buffer to other cells across distances or dimensions.

§6Crafting§f:
§eCrafted by surrounding a §bPowercell (Low)§f in the center with §b4× Power Core (Medium)§f in cardinal directions, and §b4× Redstone§f in the corners.

§eTip§f: When upgrading, the Copy NBT recipe preserves 100% of stored RF — plan a low-power moment for the swap so you can hand-carry the buffer up to your new Medium network without draining it.
